---
title: "Paper III — Tidal Ionization and the Quantum Roche Limit"
description: >-
  A third ionization channel (gradient detuning) alongside the
  standard thermal (Saha) and pressure (Pauli) channels. Predicts
  an atomic ISCO with two observational signatures. In preparation.
draft: true
---

<!--
  Draft landing page for Paper III. Does not render to the live
  site while `draft: true` is set.

  Paper III is itself at v0.1 (draft) — no Zenodo DOI yet. This
  landing page is forward-looking; sections describe what the paper
  will establish rather than what it has established. When v1.0 is
  deposited, rewrite the body in past tense, add the DOI, and
  remove `draft: true`.
-->

::: {.callout-note}
Paper III is **in preparation** (v0.1 draft). No Zenodo DOI yet;
the working repository is the active development surface.
:::

## Planned contribution

A third ionization channel — **gradient detuning** — alongside the
two standard channels:

- **Saha (thermal) ionization.** <One sentence locating it.>
- **Pauli (pressure) ionization.** <One sentence.>
- **Gradient-detuning ionization** (Paper III). <One sentence on
  the mechanism: how a local clock-density gradient detunes the
  resonance condition that holds the atom together.>

## Headline prediction

An **atomic ISCO** (innermost stable circular orbit, by analogy
with the gravitational ISCO): a radius inside which atoms cannot
remain bound regardless of temperature or pressure. Two
observational signatures:

1. **21 cm HI inner-edge morphology** near compact objects.
2. **Fe K$\alpha$ line-profile inner edge** in accretion disks.

<One paragraph on what each signature would look like and why
existing data is consistent with both predictions but does not
yet test them at the resolution Paper III requires.>

## Methodological role

Third step in the arc, observational predictions side: the
framework's first **falsifiable astrophysical prediction**. <One
paragraph on how this differs from Papers I and II (which were
derivational / structural) and what it adds to the methodological
case.>

## Connection to dcl_core

Paper III uses [`dcl_core`](dcl-core.qmd) for its numerical
experiments — specifically the side-by-side comparison of
`dcl_core.core` (continuous-amplitude) and `dcl_core.core3d`
(integer-token) on minimum-momentum-uncertainty and
Arnold-tongue frequency-locking scans. <Expand once experiments
are finalized.>

## Current state

- **Version:** v0.1 (draft)
- **Repository:** [JackDMenendez/dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization)
- **DOI:** *not yet deposited; will be assigned at v1.0.*

## What unlocks v1.0

<Short bullet list of the remaining work items that gate the v1.0
deposit — derivation closures, numerical experiments, observational
data fits. Mirrors the project board for Paper III.>
